{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should I apply? A) Light copyedit only (grammar, clarity, concision) without reframing claims or reordering sections; or B) Substantive rewrite including conceptual reframing of the claims (R1.1), tightening the internal logic (R1.2), and possible section-level reorganization (R1.5).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Ensure the internal argument chain is coherent and non-contradictory as the draft advances causal explanations and hypotheses across algorithms.",
    "R1.6": "Polish sentence- and paragraph-level wording for clarity, flow, and concision to make the results and claims easier to read.",
    "RX.1": "Flag and preserve appropriate hedging where results are hypothesis-driven or context-dependent.",
    "RX.2": "Avoid strengthening claims beyond what the excerpt states about observed performance and hypotheses.",
    "RX.3": "Constrain work to improving the given draft without introducing new analyses or external content.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid edits that could encourage unsafe or misleading deployment interpretations in high-stakes settings.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain traceability to the original statements and note where any substantive changes to meaning would occur."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should I apply? A) Light copyedit only (grammar, clarity, concision) without reframing claims or reordering sections; or B) Substantive rewrite including conceptual reframing of the claims (R1.1), tightening the internal logic (R1.2), and possible section-level reorganization (R1.5).

rationale.R1.2: Ensure the internal argument chain is coherent and non-contradictory as the draft advances causal explanations and hypotheses across algorithms.
rationale.R1.6: Polish sentence- and paragraph-level wording for clarity, flow, and concision to make the results and claims easier to read.
rationale.RX.1: Flag and preserve appropriate hedging where results are hypothesis-driven or context-dependent.
rationale.RX.2: Avoid strengthening claims beyond what the excerpt states about observed performance and hypotheses.
rationale.RX.3: Constrain work to improving the given draft without introducing new analyses or external content.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid edits that could encourage unsafe or misleading deployment interpretations in high-stakes settings.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain traceability to the original statements and note where any substantive changes to meaning would occur.
